title: in MXE build abc9 crashes on read <abc-temp-dir>/input.xaig
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue
Compiled 143f82d with 32-bit and 64-bit MXE, run in Win 7.
Used simple 4-bit counter:

[counter4.zip](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/3661661/counter4.zip)

As a target used xc7 with commands:
>read_verilog counter4.v
synth_xilinx -family xc7 -abc9 -top counter4 -edif proj_xilinx.edf

## Expected behavior

In xubuntu 18.04 with 64-bit build block compiles successfully for the same 143f82d tag.
>2.39.1.4. Executing ABC9.
Running ABC command: <yosys-exe-dir>/yosys-abc -s -f <abc-temp-dir>/abc.script 2>&1
ABC: ABC command line: "source <abc-temp-dir>/abc.script".
ABC: 
ABC: + read_lut <yosys-exe-dir>/share/xilinx/abc_xc7.lut 
ABC: + read_box -v <yosys-exe-dir>/share/xilinx/abc_xc7.box 
ABC: + &read <abc-temp-dir>/input.xaig 
ABC: + &ps 
ABC: <abc-temp-dir>/input : i/o =      6/     10  and =       0  lev =    0 (0.00)  mem = 0.00 MB  box = 1  bb = 0
ABC: + &st 
ABC: + &scorr 
ABC: Warning: The network is combinational.
ABC: + &sweep 
ABC: + &dc2 
ABC: + &st 
ABC: + &dch -f 
ABC: + &ps 
ABC: <abc-temp-dir>/input : i/o =      6/     10  and =       0  lev =    0 (0.00)  mem = 0.00 MB  ch =    0  box = 1  bb = 0
ABC: + &if -W 300 -v 
ABC: K = 8. Memory (bytes): Truth =    0. Cut =   64. Obj =  144. Set =  672. CutMin = no
ABC: Node =       0.  Ch =     0.  Total mem =    0.01 MB. Peak cut mem =    0.00 MB.
ABC: P:  Del =  921.00.  Ar =       0.0.  Edge =        0.  Cut =        0.  T =     0.00 sec
ABC: P:  Del =  921.00.  Ar =       0.0.  Edge =        0.  Cut =        0.  T =     0.00 sec
ABC: P:  Del =  921.00.  Ar =       0.0.  Edge =        0.  Cut =        0.  T =     0.00 sec
ABC: F:  Del =  921.00.  Ar =       0.0.  Edge =        0.  Cut =        0.  T =     0.00 sec
ABC: A:  Del =  921.00.  Ar =       0.0.  Edge =        0.  Cut =        0.  T =     0.00 sec
ABC: A:  Del =  921.00.  Ar =       0.0.  Edge =        0.  Cut =        0.  T =     0.00 sec
ABC: Total time =     0.00 sec
ABC: + &mfs 
ABC: Error: Abc_CommandAbc9Mfs(): The current white-boxes have more than 6 inputs. Cannot use "mfs".
ABC: + &ps -l 
ABC: <abc-temp-dir>/input : i/o =      6/     10  and =       0  lev =    0 (0.00)  mem = 0.00 MB  box = 1  bb = 0
ABC: Mapping (K=0)  :  lut =      0  edge =       0  lev =    0 (0.00)  levB =    1  mem = 0.00 MB
ABC: LUT = 0 : Ave = 0.00
ABC: + &write <abc-temp-dir>/output.aig 
<suppressed ~41 debug messages>
Removed 0 unused cells and 30 unused wires.


## Actual behavior

I've compiled tags 143f82d 0a2d8db and 903cd58 with both 32-bit and 64-bit MXE builds in xubuntu 18.04. In 64-bit build ABC was without pthread support.
All of them cause ABC to exit on assertion:

>2.39.1.4. Executing ABC9.
Running ABC command: <yosys-exe-dir>/yosys-abc -s -f <abc-temp-dir>/abc.script 2>&1
ABC: ABC command line: "source <abc-temp-dir>/abc.script".
ABC: 
ABC: + read_lut <yosys-exe-dir>/share\xilinx/abc_xc7.lut 
ABC: + read_box -v <yosys-exe-dir>/share\xilinx/abc_xc7.box 
ABC: + &read <abc-temp-dir>/input.xaig 
ABC: 
ABC: This application has requested the Runtime to terminate it in an unusual way.
ABC: Please contact the application's support team for more information.
ABC: Assertion failed!
ABC: 
ABC: Program: <yosys-exe-dir>/yosys-abc.exe
ABC: File: ./src/misc/vec/vecStr.h, Line 356
ABC: 
ABC: Expression: i >= 0 && i < p->nSize_

input.xaig is included
[input.zip](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/3661632/input.zip)
